text: A three-dimensional network solid containing Fe(III) sites and paramagnetic semiquinoid ligands (NBu4)2Fe(III)2(dhbq)3 (where dhbq(2-/3-) denotes 2,5-dioxidobenzoquinone/1,2-dioxido-4,5-semiquinone) with a conductivity at 298 K of 0.16 ± 0.01 S/cm is herein reported. It is currently among the highest reported for a metal-organic framework (MOF). Upon detailed investigation into the electronic conduction behavior of this compound, it is demonstrated that the conduction in this material stems from ligand mixed-valency. A full characterization of the mixed-valency behavior using a range of techniques, which include UV-vis-NIR diffuse reflectance spectroscopy, slow-scan cyclic voltammetry and variable-temperature conductivity and magnetic susceptibility measurements, is reported. The first instance of UV-vis-NIR diffuse reflectance measurement of Robin-Day Class II/III mixed valency in a MOF is also herein reported. Furthermore, careful stoichiometric control of the redox behavior of the dhbq6- ligands afforded the reduced framework material Na0.9(NBu4)1.8Fe(III)2(dhbq)3. A detailed investigation of the electronic and magnetic behavior, as a function of the ratio of the mixed-valent states, within the two framework materials is given. The transition metal-semiquinoid system is thereby confirmed as one of the prime candidates for imparting tunable long-range electronic communication within MOFs.
